frontmatter.description: Captured half of a chat transcript with embedded code fences that often trip downstream content validators.

asset.body:
Chat fragment captured mid-debug:

> alice: did you ever fix the deploy thing?
> me: not really
> alice: ```
> alice: maybe try restarting the worker?

Some of the code I was testing:

```python
def foo(x):
    return x +

# never finished pasting this
```

and a stray heading
## description

then more text without context.

feedback.reason: Unfinished code snippets and a stray `## description` heading make this prone to producing malformed lesson output
feedback.note: Last time it was distilled the description field was a heading reference rather than a sentence.

feedback.reason: Embedded `## description` heading is the exact antipattern the LESSON_SYSTEM_PROMPT calls out (BAD: 'Key pitfalls')

humanGrade.rationale: Source contains the documented antipattern (a heading literally named `description`). A model that follows the structured prompt will likely copy the heading into the description field, which the lesson lint rejects. validation_failed is the most likely realized outcome; a quality_rejected within the same low band is acceptable variance.
